name: Flutter Web
description: Google's cross-platform UI toolkit for building natively compiled web applications from a single Dart codebase

description.value: Build beautiful, natively compiled web apps from a single Dart codebase

initial-release.comment: Flutter released May 2017; web support became stable in March 2021

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained by Google with quarterly releases; 825 commits from 145 contributors in 3.38

reactivity-model.comment: Flutter uses widget rebuild with setState() triggering UI updates; similar to dirty checking pattern

component-model.comment: Widgets are Dart classes extending StatelessWidget or StatefulWidget

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in setState for local state; Provider (official), Riverpod, and BLoC for complex apps

opinionatedness.comment: Everything is a widget; strongly prescriptive architecture with Material and Cupertino design systems

backward-compatibility.comment: Google maintains strong backward compatibility with migration guides for breaking changes

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to JavaScript or WebAssembly; WASM support available with flutter build web --wasm

typescript-support.comment: Dart is the primary language with its own strong type system; TypeScript not applicable

template-syntax.comment: Dart code as UI; widget tree built using Dart constructors and composition

styling-approach.comment: Styling via widget properties and theme data; no CSS, uses Dart code for all styling

build-required.comment: Requires Flutter SDK and build process

wasm-interop.comment: JS interop via dart:js_interop and package:js for calling JavaScript from Dart

reactive-primitives.comment: No built-in signals/observables; uses setState, ChangeNotifier, or external state management

computed-values.comment: No reactive computed primitives; computed values done via getters or state management libraries

two-way-binding.comment: No built-in two-way binding; form fields use TextEditingController with explicit listeners

event-handling.comment: GestureDetector, onPressed, onTap, and other event callbacks on widgets

conditional-rendering.comment: Dart ternary operators and if statements in widget tree

list-rendering.comment: ListView.builder, .map() on collections for efficient list rendering

component-slots.comment: Child/children widget parameters for content projection

teleport-portal.comment: OverlayPortal and Overlay for rendering outside the normal widget tree

suspense.comment: No Suspense-like boundaries; FutureBuilder and StreamBuilder handle async loading states

error-boundaries.comment: ErrorWidget.builder and FlutterError.onError for global error handling

context-provide-inject.comment: InheritedWidget and Provider for dependency injection down the widget tree

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Flutter Web renders to Canvas/WASM, not DOM; no direct DOM access by design

official-router.comment: go_router is officially maintained by the Flutter team

route-guards.comment: Redirect function for authentication guards and route protection

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Deferred components allow lazy loading of parts of the app

scroll-restoration.comment: Browser History API integration maintains scroll position

built-in-state.comment: setState() for StatefulWidget local state management

built-in-global-state.comment: Provider is official but separate package; no global state in core Flutter

global-state-library.comment: Provider is officially recommended; Riverpod and BLoC are popular alternatives

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Flutter DevTools includes state inspection for widgets

state-persistence.comment: Requires manual implementation with shared_preferences or hive packages

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not built-in; some state management libraries like redux may provide this

official-cli.comment: flutter CLI with 'flutter create' for project scaffolding

hot-module-replacement.comment: Hot reload for development; experimental web hot reload in Flutter 3.38

browser-devtools.comment: Flutter DevTools runs in browser; Dart Debug Extension for Chrome

vs-code-extension.comment: Official Flutter and Dart extensions for VS Code

error-messages.comment: Helpful error messages with suggestions; Flutter Analyzer provides good diagnostics

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent documentation with tutorials, codelabs, API reference, and interactive examples

playground.comment: Official DartPad for experimenting with Dart and Flutter code

bundle-size-core.comment: ~530KB with HTML renderer; CanvasKit adds ~1.5MB WASM file

bundle-size-full.comment: ~2MB+ with CanvasKit renderer (recommended); includes Skia WASM runtime

virtual-dom.comment: Renders directly to Canvas (CanvasKit) or DOM elements (HTML renderer), not virtual DOM

compile-time-optimization.comment: Dart compiler performs tree shaking and optimization

tree-shaking.comment: Dart compiler removes unused code; --tree-shake-icons for icon fonts

code-splitting.comment: Deferred components for web code splitting

lazy-components.comment: Deferred loading with Dart's deferred as keyword

ssr-capable.comment: No native SSR support; third-party solutions like Jaspr exist for Dart web SSR

streaming-ssr.comment: Not supported; Flutter Web is client-side only

partial-hydration.comment: Not supported; Flutter Web renders entire widget tree

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Flutter Web not typically included in js-framework-benchmark; Canvas-based rendering makes comparison difficult

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Flutter uses pub.dev, not npm. The flutter npm package has ~275 downloads but is not the actual framework

component-libraries.comment: Built-in Material and Cupertino; plus third-party like Flutter Flow UI, FluentUI, GetWidget, etc.

meta-framework.comment: Flutter itself is the full-stack solution; no separate meta-framework needed

major-companies-using.value: Google, Alibaba, BMW, Toyota, eBay, Tencent

job-market-demand.comment: Growing demand; primarily for mobile but web expertise valued. ~1000 Flutter jobs vs ~6400 React Native on LinkedIn

official-testing-library.comment: flutter_test package included in Flutter SDK

component-testing.comment: Widget tests via WidgetTester with pumpWidget and finders

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Integration tests package; also works with Patrol and other E2E tools

snapshot-testing.comment: Golden file testing with matchesGoldenFile matcher

mobile-framework.comment: Flutter itself is the mobile framework; same codebase for iOS, Android, and web

pwa-support.comment: PWA template with manifest.json and service worker included

desktop-framework.comment: Flutter natively supports Windows, macOS, and Linux desktop apps

learning-curve.comment: Requires learning Dart, widget composition, state management patterns; steeper for JS developers but fast for mobile devs

concepts-to-learn.comment: Dart basics, widgets, StatelessWidget/StatefulWidget, BuildContext, state management, layouts, navigation, themes, platform channels, lifecycle

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes with Flutter SDK installed; flutter create and flutter run --release -d chrome

migration-path.comment: Guides for developers coming from iOS, Android, React Native, Xamarin, and web development

incremental-adoption.comment: Flutter Web is all-or-nothing; cannot incrementally add to existing JS web apps
